title: A machine learning model for drought tracking and forecasting using remote precipitation data and a standardized precipitation index from arid regions
data_target: CHIRPS precipitation; SPI at multiple timescales; reported forecasting focuses on annual SPI-12
forecast_design: Annual drought/SPI-12 prediction from lagged SPI and precipitation
predictors_or_inputs: Lagged SPI and precipitation; Extreme Learning Machine models
validation_and_metrics: Reported R2 around 0.7-0.8 in public abstract/summary; probabilistic BSS and climatology-relative monthly dry-fraction skill not the primary framing
comparability_to_this_project: Low direct comparability: CHIRPS and SPI are shared, but SPI-12 annual prediction is smoother and higher-memory than monthly SPI-1 dry-fraction probability
protocol_assessment: Do not call this leaky from available summary. Treat as a different target/metric design that can succeed where SPI-1 BSS does not.
claim_use: Use to explain why CHIRPS/SPI papers can report high skill without contradicting our SPI-1 result.

title: Spatiotemporal characterization of drought in Botswana: a regionalization study using long-term CHIRPS satellite data
data_target: CHIRPS; long-term SPI drought characterization and regionalization
predictors_or_inputs: SPI regionalization, run-theory drought metrics, teleconnection diagnostics
validation_and_metrics: Mechanism and regionalization diagnostics; not evaluated as lead-time probabilistic BSS against climatology
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability for mechanism analysis only; not comparable as a forecast-skill claim
protocol_assessment: No leakage critique needed because it is not presented as our type of forecast model.
claim_use: Use as source for CHIRPS SPI regionalization methods and mechanism framing.

title: Prediction of meteorological drought and standardized precipitation index based on RF, RT, and GPR models
data_target: Station or basin meteorological drought; SPI prediction
predictors_or_inputs: Random Forest, Random Tree, Gaussian Process Regression and meteorological/SPI predictors
validation_and_metrics: Standard regression metrics in public bibliographic records; climatology-relative probabilistic BSS not the central framing
comparability_to_this_project: Low to partial comparability: meteorological SPI is shared, but target construction, lead design, and deterministic metrics differ from monthly dry-fraction BSS
protocol_assessment: Do not infer leakage without full protocol audit. Classify as not directly comparable to our strict probabilistic benchmark.
claim_use: Use as example of common deterministic SPI-prediction literature that requires careful task comparison.

title: Short lead time standard precipitation index forecasting: Extreme learning machine and variational mode decomposition
forecast_design: Short-lead SPI forecasting with decomposition plus ELM
predictors_or_inputs: Variational mode decomposition, ELM, SPI input combinations
validation_and_metrics: Deterministic prediction metrics; public summary emphasizes improved SPI forecasting accuracy
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability: short-lead SPI forecasting is related, but decomposition of the target series and deterministic metrics differ from leakage-safe probability forecasting
protocol_assessment: Potential target-decomposition methods need careful lead audit before comparison; do not claim invalidity from abstract alone.
claim_use: Use as a contrast showing that signal-processing plus SPI-memory targets are a different forecast family.

title: Seasonal Drought Forecasting for Latin America Using the ECMWF S4 Forecast System
data_target: Seasonal precipitation forecasts transformed to SPI; Latin America
forecast_design: Operational seasonal SPI forecasts from ECMWF S4
predictors_or_inputs: Dynamical model precipitation forecasts, not lagged observations only
validation_and_metrics: Skill evaluated relative to climatological benchmarks; regional, season, lead, and SPI-scale dependence emphasized
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability: strong benchmark for seasonal SPI verification, but uses dynamical forecast inputs and broader seasonal targets
protocol_assessment: This meets the spirit of forecast-informed verification better than lag-only ML; it should not be grouped with CHIRPS-lag studies.
claim_use: Use to justify climatology-relative seasonal SPI verification and conditional skill framing.

title: Probabilistic Seasonal Forecasts in the North American Multimodel Ensemble: A Baseline Skill Assessment
validation_and_metrics: Brier skill score, reliability, resolution, and cross-validated climatology baseline
comparability_to_this_project: High comparability for verification philosophy, low comparability for CHIRPS SPI-1 target
protocol_assessment: Good standard for probabilistic verification; supports our BSS/reliability framing.
claim_use: Use as the core source for BSS/reliability/resolution benchmarking.

title: Evaluation of Subseasonal Drought Forecast Skill over the Coastal Western United States
data_target: SubX-driven drought onset and termination over coastal western U.S.
forecast_design: Subseasonal event forecasts at weekly leads
predictors_or_inputs: SubX model forcing and land-surface drought-event definitions
validation_and_metrics: Probabilistic drought onset/termination skill; public/local notes indicate useful skill mainly weeks 1-2, limited by week 3, and weak by week 4 for many cases
comparability_to_this_project: High conceptual comparability for lead-time limits and event targets; target/input system differs from our monthly CHIRPS SPI-1
protocol_assessment: Strong support for cautious 1-month predictability framing; not evidence that CHIRPS itself is flawed.
claim_use: Use to support month-ahead difficulty and onset/termination diagnostics.

title: Subseasonal Forecast Skill of Evaporative Demand, Soil Moisture, and Flash Drought Onset from Two Dynamic Models over CONUS
data_target: SubX ETo, RZSM, flash-drought indices over CONUS
predictors_or_inputs: GEOS-V2p1 and GEFSv12 SubX forecasts; land-surface variables
validation_and_metrics: Skill declines by lead and varies by target; evaluates against multiple reference datasets
comparability_to_this_project: High conceptual comparability for land-surface forecast benchmarks, but not for CHIRPS-only SPI-1
protocol_assessment: Supports dynamic-model and land-surface benchmark direction; no leakage critique.
claim_use: Use to justify why root-zone soil moisture and dynamic forecasts are the next positive-skill path.

title: Skillful subseasonal soil moisture drought forecasts with deep learning-dynamic models
data_target: Root-zone soil moisture drought forecast target
forecast_design: Subseasonal DL plus dynamic-model forecast hybrid
predictors_or_inputs: Antecedent RZSM and dynamic-model forecasts; RISE-UNet
validation_and_metrics: Reports skillful RZSM forecasts at subseasonal leads; benchmarked as a modern forecast-informed land-surface task
comparability_to_this_project: Low direct comparability to CHIRPS SPI-1; high relevance for future extension
protocol_assessment: Shows positive results usually require a target/input pivot, not just a larger network on CHIRPS lags.
claim_use: Use to support the land-surface target-reframing hypothesis.

title: Improving Subseasonal Forecasting in the Western U.S. with Machine Learning
data_target: Western U.S. subseasonal temperature and precipitation
forecast_design: Forecast Rodeo style 2-6 week forecast competition
predictors_or_inputs: Meteorological observations, climate indices, dynamical model forecasts, geography, and benchmark features
validation_and_metrics: Skill relative to contest/benchmark systems for temperature and precipitation
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability for western U.S. precipitation lead-time problem; not a drought-index dry-fraction BSS task
protocol_assessment: Successful ML here is forecast-informed and benchmark-heavy; not evidence that CHIRPS-only SPI-1 should be skillful.
claim_use: Use to justify adding operational forecast predictors only if pursuing positive skill.

title: SubseasonalClimateUSA: A Dataset for Subseasonal Forecasting and Benchmarking
data_target: CONUS subseasonal temperature and precipitation benchmark dataset
forecast_design: Benchmark dataset and model comparison at weeks 3-4 and 5-6
predictors_or_inputs: Operational dynamical forecasts, meteorological baselines, and ML/deep learning methods
validation_and_metrics: Explicit benchmark-first comparison including climatology, persistence, and operational models
comparability_to_this_project: High relevance for benchmark design; target differs from drought SPI dry fraction
protocol_assessment: Supports standardized baselines and dynamic forecast inputs; no claim about CHIRPS leakage.

title: DroughtCast: A Machine Learning Forecast of the United States Drought Monitor
predictors_or_inputs: Precipitation, soil moisture, temperature, remote sensing, and related environmental predictors
validation_and_metrics: Forecast evaluation for USDM target; includes spatial and temporal generalization concerns in local notes
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability: drought target is operational and multi-driver, not CHIRPS SPI-1
protocol_assessment: Positive drought-ML examples often use richer targets and predictors than our canonical setup.
claim_use: Use to contextualize why our lag-only SPI-1 task is intentionally narrower.

title: DroughtSet: Understanding Drought Through Spatial-Temporal Learning
predictors_or_inputs: Weekly climate-related variables, static data, and spatial-temporal learning models
validation_and_metrics: Benchmark-oriented ML evaluation; public abstract frames dataset for drought prediction research
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability as drought ML benchmark; richer data and target setup than this project
protocol_assessment: Supports the idea that state-of-the-art drought ML is moving toward multi-driver benchmark datasets.
claim_use: Use to position our work as a stricter targeted audit, not a general benchmark replacement.

title: Explainable AI to explore climatic parameters governing meteorological drought forecasting
predictors_or_inputs: Lagged drought-index and climate predictors; SHAP/LIME interpretation
validation_and_metrics: Predictive metrics plus interpretability; local notes indicate lagged index terms can dominate
comparability_to_this_project: Partial comparability for interpretability and lagged-index dominance; different target/evaluation details
protocol_assessment: Use cautiously: SHAP supports model-behavior interpretation, not causal proof.
claim_use: Use to justify SHAP as mechanism/audit evidence only.

title: Hyperparameter tuning and performance assessment of statistical and machine-learning algorithms using spatial data
forecast_design: Spatial validation and performance assessment, not drought-specific
validation_and_metrics: Shows random/non-spatial resampling can produce overoptimistic performance under spatial autocorrelation
comparability_to_this_project: High relevance for validation risk; not a drought forecast paper
protocol_assessment: Supports our spatial pseudo-replication concern without accusing individual drought papers of leakage.
claim_use: Use as external methodological support for blocked/monthly inference and spatial-autocorrelation guardrails.
